# Palm Leaf Partners

> A DRC-rooted infrastructure and technology firm building the foundational systems — national address mapping, sovereign data centers, regional emergency call centers, and fiber backbone — that the Democratic Republic of the Congo needs to grow. 25-year partnership with SCPT, the Congolese national post and telecommunications company.

Palm Leaf Partners (PLP) is a Wyoming LLC (USA). Its DRC operations are conducted through UPTWB SAS, a DRC-registered Société par Actions Simplifiée under OHADA Uniform Acts and the DRC's Public-Private Partnership Law 20/018 (2020). UPTW USA, LLC provides USA-side fund management. The partnership counterparty is SCPT — Société Congolaise des Postes et Télécommunications.

Tagline: Building Systems. Growing Futures.

## Pages

- [Home](https://palmleafpartners.com/): Mission overview, four-pillar values (Growth, Partnership, Empowerment, DRC Proud), project tiles
- [National Address Mapping](https://palmleafpartners.com/pages/mapping.html): Target of up to 20 million addresses at a 95% accuracy goal, up to 100 modernized post offices with Starlink (figures indicative, subject to Scope Validation Survey and Definitive Agreement)
- [Sovereign Data Center](https://palmleafpartners.com/pages/data-center.html): Planned two-site cloud designed to Uptime Institute Tier IV standards — Kinshasa primary, Lubumbashi disaster recovery (certification on commissioning)
- [Regional Call Centers](https://palmleafpartners.com/pages/call-centers.html): Five to ten planned regional emergency-dispatch + multi-service centers in six languages (French, Lingala, Swahili, Kikongo, Tshiluba, English) — subject to Definitive Agreement and DRC regulatory approvals
- [National Fiber Backbone](https://palmleafpartners.com/pages/fiber-backbone.html): Planned fiber connecting Kinshasa, Lubumbashi, Goma, Kisangani, Mbuji-Mayi, Bukavu, with Starlink + microwave reach to 100+ remote sites (routes indicative, subject to Definitive Agreement)
- [Memorial City](https://palmleafpartners.com/pages/memorial-city.html): A planned memorial and cultural city honoring the Congolese people
- [About](https://palmleafpartners.com/pages/about.html): Mission, values, the Zola mascot, brand family including Universal Palm Tree Women Organization
- [Partnership & Governance](https://palmleafpartners.com/pages/partnership.html): Legal framework, MOUs, governance structure
- [Contact](https://palmleafpartners.com/pages/contact.html): Inquiry form for investors, governments, partners, and press

## Mascot

Zola is the brand's symbolic figure — a Congolese woman in green ceremonial dress with a palm-leaf headdress. She represents the firm's roots in the DRC and its commitment to building sustainable infrastructure and empowering communities, especially women.

## Sister organization

Universal Palm Tree Women Organization — empowering women, transforming communities. Operates alongside Palm Leaf Partners under the shared theme "Shared Roots. Shared Purpose."
